pi_items.source: export_result.sh explicitly writes the JSON result to /tmp/grocery_result.json
Task description aligns with this occupation.

Task description and export_result.sh confirm this path.
setup_task.sh PL/SQL block and verifier.py strictly calculate and enforce a lift of 13.3333 for pair 1 based on 10,000 total orders, directly contradicting the 6.6667 lift claim.
setup_task.sh PL/SQL block and verifier.py strictly calculate and enforce a lift of 44.4444 for pair 2 based on 10,000 total orders, directly contradicting the 22.2222 lift claim.

privileged_info_summary: I verified that the task operates on 10,000 synthetically generated grocery orders, populated via a PL/SQL script in setup_task.sh. I discovered that the lift values claimed in the metadata are exactly half of their true mathematical values: for pair 1 (Hot Dogs & Buns), the true lift is 13.3333 (contradicting the 6.6667 claim), and for pair 2 (Peanut Butter & Jelly), the true lift is 44.4444 (contradicting the 22.2222 claim). These true lift values of 13.3333 and 44.4444 are explicitly enforced by the scoring logic in verifier.py.
